Efficiency Trial Annual QC

Published

June 17, 2026

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(here)
library(glue)
library(DT)

if (file.exists(here::here("DESCRIPTION"))) {
  devtools::load_all(here::here(), quiet = TRUE)
} else {
  library(SRJPEdata)
}

source(here::here("data-raw", "qc", "qc_helpers.R"))

review_run_year <- params$review_run_year

Overview

Reviewing efficiency trial data. Run year being reviewed: 2026.

Data sources: weekly_efficiency (raw trial data), weekly_juvenile_abundance_efficiency_data (model-ready), years_to_exclude_rst_data.

Check 1: Recaptures Exceeding Releases

Flag trials where number_recaptured > number_released. These rows are already filtered out during the model data build, but logging them creates a traceable record for upstream correction.

Code 
recap_exceed <- eff |>
  filter(number_recaptured > number_released) |>
  group_by(stream, site, run_year) |>
  summarise(
    n_records    = n(),
    max_ratio    = round(max(number_recaptured / number_released), 2),
    .groups      = "drop"
  )
Warning: There was 1 warning in `summarise()`.
ℹ In argument: `max_ratio = round(max(number_recaptured/number_released), 2)`.
Caused by warning in `max()`:
! no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf
Code 
new_recap_exceed <- log_issues(
  recap_exceed |>
    mutate(
      data_type   = "efficiency",
      issue_type  = "recaptures_exceed_releases",
      field       = "number_recaptured",
      severity    = "critical",
      description = glue("{n_records} trial(s) in run year {run_year} at {site} ({stream}) have more recaptures than releases. Max recapture/release ratio: {max_ratio}. These rows are filtered at build time.")
    )
)
  No issues detected for this check.

No trials with recaptures exceeding releases.

Check 2: Low Release Count

Flag trials where fewer than 50 fish were released. Small releases produce high-variance efficiency estimates.

Code 
low_release <- eff |>
  filter(number_released < 50) |>
  group_by(stream, site, run_year) |>
  summarise(
    n_records   = n(),
    min_release = min(number_released),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

new_low_release <- log_issues(
  low_release |>
    mutate(
      data_type   = "efficiency",
      issue_type  = "low_release_count",
      field       = "number_released",
      severity    = "minor",
      description = glue("{n_records} trial(s) with <50 fish released in run year {run_year} at {site} ({stream}). Minimum release count: {min_release}")
    )
)
  + 6 new issue(s) logged. 0 already in log.

7 trial(s) with <50 releases flagged.

Check 3: Zero Recaptures

Flag trials with zero recaptures. Can be real (very low efficiency) but may also indicate a data entry issue.

Code 
zero_recap <- eff |>
  filter(number_recaptured == 0) |>
  group_by(stream, site, run_year) |>
  summarise(
    n_records = n(),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

new_zero_recap <- log_issues(
  zero_recap |>
    mutate(
      data_type   = "efficiency",
      issue_type  = "zero_recaptures",
      field       = "number_recaptured",
      severity    = "minor",
      description = glue("{n_records} trial(s) with 0 recaptures in run year {run_year} at {site} ({stream})")
    )
)
  + 57 new issue(s) logged. 0 already in log.

127 zero-recapture trial(s) flagged.

Check 4: Trial Coverage Per Run Year

Flag stream/site/run_years with fewer than 3 efficiency trials in the BTSPAS window (weeks 45–53 and 1–22). Indicates sparse coverage that may limit model reliability.

Code 
trial_coverage <- weekly_juvenile_abundance_efficiency_data |>
  filter(week %in% c(45:53, 1:22)) |>
  group_by(stream, site, run_year) |>
  summarise(n_trials = n(), .groups = "drop")

excluded_lookup <- years_to_exclude_rst_data |>
  distinct(stream, site, run_year) |>
  mutate(already_excluded = TRUE)

low_coverage <- trial_coverage |>
  filter(n_trials < 3) |>
  left_join(excluded_lookup, by = c("stream", "site", "run_year")) |>
  mutate(already_excluded = replace_na(already_excluded, FALSE))

new_low_coverage <- log_issues(
  low_coverage |>
    mutate(
      n_records   = n_trials,
      data_type   = "efficiency",
      issue_type  = "low_trial_coverage",
      field       = "n_trials",
      severity    = "moderate",
      description = glue("Only {n_trials} efficiency trial(s) in BTSPAS window for run year {run_year} at {site} ({stream}). Already excluded from modeling: {already_excluded}")
    )
)
  + 21 new issue(s) logged. 0 already in log.

21 site/run-year combination(s) flagged (2 already excluded from modeling).

Check 5: Efficiency Rate Outliers

For each site with at least 5 years of history, flag run_years where the mean simple efficiency (number_recaptured / number_released) is more than 3 SD above the site historical mean.

Code 
eff_rates <- eff |>
  filter(number_released > 0, number_recaptured <= number_released) |>
  mutate(simple_eff = number_recaptured / number_released) |>
  group_by(stream, site, run_year) |>
  summarise(mean_eff = mean(simple_eff, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

site_eff_stats <- eff_rates |>
  group_by(stream, site) |>
  summarise(
    n_years  = n(),
    hist_mean = mean(mean_eff, na.rm = TRUE),
    hist_sd   = sd(mean_eff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(n_years >= 5)

eff_outliers <- eff_rates |>
  inner_join(site_eff_stats, by = c("stream", "site")) |>
  filter(mean_eff > hist_mean + 3 * hist_sd)

new_eff_outliers <- log_issues(
  eff_outliers |>
    mutate(
      n_records   = 1L,
      data_type   = "efficiency",
      issue_type  = "extreme_value",
      field       = "number_recaptured",
      severity    = "minor",
      description = glue("Mean efficiency of {round(mean_eff, 3)} in run year {run_year} at {site} ({stream}) is >3 SD above site historical mean ({round(hist_mean, 3)}, SD = {round(hist_sd, 3)})")
    )
)
  No issues detected for this check.

No efficiency rate outliers detected.
